scoring: Each criterion is pass/fail. The bundle is LISTA only if every blocking criterion passes.

criteria.statement: The accept/reject threshold is applied on CALIBRATED confidence fitted against the labeled set, never on raw_confidence.
criteria.fail_signal: Cut on raw_confidence; threshold compared across model versions.

criteria.statement: Sampling is stratified by document_type with a minimum per stratum; rare strata remain visible in the metric.
criteria.fail_signal: Single global random sample; rare strata diluted out.

criteria.statement: Each category has a categorical criterion with +/- examples per severity level.
criteria.fail_signal: Vague guidance such as 'be conservative' with no +/- examples.

criteria.statement: False-positive rate is reported per category, not only as an aggregate accuracy.
criteria.fail_signal: Single aggregate accuracy presented as primary metric.

criteria.statement: A temporal disable exists for high-FP categories instead of raising the global threshold.
criteria.fail_signal: Global threshold raised to mask one noisy category.

criteria.statement: A suspended category is listed as 'suspended' (not 0% FP) and a non-calibrated stratum is not counted as a valid metric.
criteria.fail_signal: Disabled category shown as healthy; empty accepted bucket shown as perfect precision.

criteria.statement: The calibration map is non-empty and monotonic before being applied.
Thresholds are set only when a human labeled set exists; otherwise the flow stops and requests it.
criteria.fail_signal: Thresholds invented without a labeled set.

criteria.statement: Every claim carries an evidence tag ([DOC] [CODIGO] [CONFIG] [INFERENCIA] [SUPUESTO]).
No model training, no raw-document labeling, no producer-prompt tuning.
criteria.fail_signal: Skill drifts into training or labeling.
